questions.question: What is the sum of 2 and 5?
What is the product of 3 and 9?
If a pencil costs $0.50, what is the cost of 12 pencils?
If a book costs $15, what is the cost of 3 books?
What is the sum of all digits in 123?
If a shirt costs $25, what is the cost of 2 shirts?
If a pair of shoes costs $40, what is the cost of 4 pairs?
What is the sum of all digits in 456?

notes.title: Definition of a Fraction
notes.content: A fraction is a part of a whole, represented by the ratio of two numbers.

notes.title: Types of Fractions
notes.content: There are two types of fractions: proper and improper fractions. Proper fractions have a numerator smaller than the denominator, while improper fractions have a numerator larger than or equal to the denominator.

notes.title: Equivalent Fractions
notes.content: Fractions that have the same value but different numerators and denominators are called equivalent fractions.

notes.title: Adding Fractions
notes.content: To add fractions, we need to have a common denominator. We can find the least common multiple (LCM) of the two denominators to get the common denominator.

notes.title: Subtracting Fractions
notes.content: To subtract fractions, we need to have a common denominator. We can find the LCM of the two denominators to get the common denominator.

notes.title: Multiplying Fractions
notes.content: To multiply fractions, we simply multiply the numerators and denominators separately.

notes.title: Dividing Fractions
notes.content: To divide fractions, we invert the second fraction and change the division to multiplication.

notes.title: Reducing Fractions
notes.content: We can simplify a fraction by dividing both the numerator and denominator by their greatest common divisor (GCD).

notes.title: Converting Mixed Numbers to Improper Fractions
notes.content: To convert a mixed number to an improper fraction, we multiply the whole number part by the denominator and add the numerator. Then, we write the result over the original denominator.

notes.title: Converting Improper Fractions to Mixed Numbers
notes.content: To convert an improper fraction to a mixed number, we divide the numerator by the denominator and write the quotient as the whole number part. The remainder becomes the new numerator.

facts.fact: The word 'fraction' comes from the Latin word 'fractus', meaning 'broken'.
Fractions were first used by ancient civilizations, such as the Egyptians and Babylonians.
The concept of equivalent fractions was understood by the ancient Greeks.
The first recorded use of algebraic methods for solving equations involving fractions dates back to the 9th century.
The notation for fractions, using a horizontal line and the slash (/), was introduced in the 16th century.
Fractions are used extensively in various fields, including mathematics, science, engineering, and finance.
The study of fractions is an essential part of mathematical education, as it provides a foundation for more advanced topics like algebra and calculus.
Fractions can be represented using various notations, including the fraction bar (/), the colon (:), and the vinculum (~).
The concept of equivalent fractions is used extensively in real-world applications, such as cooking recipes and measuring ingredients.
Fractions have numerous properties and theorems associated with them, including the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic for fractions.
